tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: Renal function shows mildly elevated serum creatinine, suggesting possible early renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), which may indicate underlying renal or urinary tract pathology. Blood glucose and HbA1c are low, but not critically so. No evidence of urinary infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Recommend further evaluation of renal function and possible nephrotic or tubular disorder; clinical correlation and follow-up advised.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
